meta.title: G26 Battery Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW i4 G26 powertrain — from eDrive35 to M60 xDrive. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 20+ vetted BMW EV specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW i4 G26 battery replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G26 battery pack costs between £12,000 and £28,000 supply-only, depending on the variant — the eDrive35's smaller 66 kWh pack ranges £12,000–£18,000, while the larger 80.7 kWh packs in the eDrive40, xDrive40, M50, and M60 run £14,000–£28,000. Motor replacement is significantly cheaper. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW i4 G26 powertrain is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The eDrive40 (340 bhp) is the most reliable i4 powertrain — it uses the larger, more durable 80.7 kWh battery pack and has the simplest single-motor RWD configuration. The eDrive35's smaller battery has shown more rapid degradation, and the M50/M60's high-performance motors place greater stress on components.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW i4 G26?

acceptedAnswer.text: Battery degradation on early eDrive35 models (2021–2022), charging port/CCS socket locking pin failure, motor bearing wear on high-performance M50/M60 variants, and battery management system software issues. BMW has released multiple software updates to address these concerns on later cars.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW i4 G26 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — BMW's fifth-generation eDrive technology is proven across the EV range. The i4 is generally reliable, with most issues being software-addressable or minor components (charging socket) rather than catastrophic battery failures. Early eDrive35 examples are the highest risk; post-2022 cars are significantly improved.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW i4 G26 variant should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early (2021–2022) eDrive35 models with the smaller 66 kWh battery and without comprehensive software updates are the highest risk. We also advise caution on high-mileage M50s without documented motor inspection. If you must have an i4, choose a 2023+ eDrive40 with full service history.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G26 battery needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Significant range loss (more than 30% degradation), rapid battery drain, charging interruption errors, or battery management system warnings are the key warning signs. BMW's iDrive system provides battery health data. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW i4 G26 battery, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold — but this is a £30,000+ car, so value assessment differs. Against a well-kept £35,000 i4, a £14,000 battery replacement is 40% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £25,000 early example, the same replacement is 56% — borderline. For sub-£20,000 examples, we'd recommend walking away.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW i4 G26 batteries last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 80.7 kWh battery in the eDrive40 and higher variants is rated for 100,000+ miles with 70-80% capacity retention. The eDrive35's smaller battery has shown more rapid degradation, typically retaining 70% capacity after 60,000–80,000 miles. BMW's battery warranty covers 8 years or 100,000 miles (70% capacity).

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G26 and other 4 Series generations?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G26 is the electric variant of the 4 Series Gran Coupé, produced since 2021. It shares its body-in-white with the combustion-engined G26 (420i, 430i, M440i) but uses BMW's fifth-generation eDrive technology. The G22 is the two-door coupé, and the G23 is the convertible — all combustion-engined variants. The i4 is BMW's first electric Gran Coupé.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW i4 G26 powertrain code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The powertrain code is listed on the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, and on BMW's service records. For eDrive35, look for HA0 / HA0001N0. For eDrive40, look for HA0 / HA0001N1. For xDrive40, look for XE2 / HA0002N0. For M50, look for HA0002N0 / HA0003N0. For M60 xDrive, look for HA0002N0 / HA0003N1. Tell us your variant and year, and we'll confirm.

hero.tagPill: BMW 4 Series G26 i4 • Gran Coupé • 2021–present
hero.h1: BMW i4 G26 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every powertrain fitted to the BMW i4 G26 — from the 282 bhp rear-drive eDrive35 to the 593 bhp all-wheel-drive M60 xDrive. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the fifth-generation eDrive technology that defines BMW's electric Gran Coupé. Backed by real UK 4 Series enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW i4 G26 Powertrain Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every powertrain fitted to the i4 G26 specifically — from the 2021 launch through to the current M60 flagship and LCI updates.

overview.intro: The BMW i4 G26 is a battery electric compact executive car produced by BMW since October 2021. It adopts a five-door liftback body style and is marketed as a four-door coupé, sharing its body-in-white with the combustion-engined 4 Series Gran Coupé. The i4 is powered by BMW's fifth-generation eDrive technology, featuring 3-phase electrically excited synchronous motors. The powertrain line-up has evolved over its production run: the eDrive35 (286 bhp) and eDrive40 (340 bhp) are rear-wheel drive; the xDrive40 (401 bhp) and M50 (544 bhp) introduced all-wheel drive; the M60 xDrive (601 bhp) replaced the M50 in 2024 as the range-topping model. A 2024 facelift (LCI) introduced updated styling and revised battery options.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (Cluster Architecture) • Predecessor: None (first electric 4 Series) • Successor: Current model — on sale • Sibling body styles: G22 Coupé, G23 Convertible — combustion-engined variants
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The i4 G26 is BMW's best-selling electric model in the UK; enquiries are concentrated on battery health, charging system diagnostics, and range concerns, with values ranging from £35,000 for early eDrive35 examples to £65,000+ for well-specced M60s.

items.engine: Early eDrive35 (2021–2022) — Early i4s (particularly 2021–2022 eDrive35 models) have documented battery management system issues and charging port failures. The smaller 66 kWh battery also suffers from more rapid degradation than the larger 80.7 kWh units.
items.whoItsFor: only buyers with comprehensive BMW warranty cover.

items.engine: M60 xDrive Battery Pack — The 80.7 kWh battery pack in the M60 is the most expensive component to replace, with costs exceeding £25,000 for a complete battery module replacement. The dual-motor configuration also adds complexity.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who accept the premium and keep the car under warranty.

items.engine: xDrive40 (401 bhp) — Often overshadowed by the M50 and M60, the 401 bhp xDrive40 delivers genuine performance (0-60 in 5.1 seconds) with all-weather traction. It's the sweet spot for UK buyers who want AWD without the M-badge premium.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want all-weather performance without the M60's running costs.

economicsRule.title: The G26 rule
economicsRule.text: A £14,000–£20,000 eDrive40 battery replacement against a £25,000–£35,000 early i4 sits at 40–80% of the car's value. On a sub-£25,000 example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £35,000+ example, the same replacement drops to a routine 40–57% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G26 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: eDrive40 — best range, most common, best parts availability.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G26 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M60 xDrive — battery replacement and motor repairs are most expensive.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G26 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: eDrive40 — the sweet spot between performance, range, and running costs.

problems.title: Battery Degradation (Early eDrive35)
problems.rootCause: The 66 kWh battery pack in early eDrive35 models suffers from more rapid capacity loss than the larger 80.7 kWh units. Frequent DC fast charging and high ambient temperatures accelerate degradation. BMW's battery management system software updates have improved longevity on later cars.

tieredCosts.work: BMS update, cell balancing, module replacement

problems.recommendation: On any early i4, have the battery health checked annually — a £300–£1,200 preventative service is cheaper than the £12,000–£18,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare G26 battery prices for your i4 →

problems.title: Charging Port/CCS Socket Failure
problems.affectedModels: All i4 G26 variants, particularly early production (2021–2022).
problems.rootCause: The Combined Charging System (CCS) socket can develop intermittent faults, particularly with the locking pin mechanism. Symptoms include charging interruptions, failure to initiate charging, and error messages. The locking pin can seize in cold weather or after frequent use.
problems.recommendation: If your i4 exhibits intermittent charging faults, have the CCS socket inspected immediately — a £300–£600 repair is cheaper than the £800–£1,500 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare G26 charging system repairs for your i4 →

problems.title: Electric Motor Bearing Wear (M50/M60)
problems.affectedModels: i4 M50 (2021–2024) and M60 xDrive (2024–present), high-performance variants.
problems.rootCause: The high-torque electric motors in M-badged i4s place significant stress on the motor bearings. A whining noise from the motor under load is the classic early warning. Ignored, bearing failure can damage the motor rotor and require complete motor replacement.

tieredCosts.work: Motor bearing replacement, cooling system service

problems.recommendation: On any M50 or M60, listen for motor whine at low speeds — a £1,000–£2,500 preventative repair is cheaper than the £6,000–£10,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare G26 motor prices for your i4 →

replacementCosts.h2: G26 Powertrain Replacement Costs — By Variant and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are G26 i4 only.
